7.1 Multiutility Payments 
(I) Multiutility Accounts  
Various  utility payments  like  (i)  Insurance  Premiums  (ii)  School  /  College  /  University  Fee, 
Telephone bills, Electricity bills, Subscription to Newspapers, Dealer payments for supply of goods 
and  other  Business  to  Business  and  Customer  to  Business  payments  can  be  collected  in  the 
multiutility accounts under these products.  
 
For multiutility account products, the system will not charge (i) Inter-city transaction charges  
(ii) Minimum balance charges (iii) Folio charges (iv) Cheque Book issue charges (v) Cheque Return 
charges etc.,  
 
The advantages of these products are  
a. Payer ID details  like Student / Dealer No / Agent code / Subscriber No etc. and  other MIS 
information required by the Corporate / Institutions for reconciliation purpose can be captured 
without  omission. System  will  not  permit  any  credit  in  these  accounts  without  providing  the 
information required for reconciliation. Customer can withdraw money in these accounts.  
b. Report / text file can be provided to them for reconciliation of amounts with the Statement of 
Account provided by us.  
c. Amount can be collected by any branch and the amount will be automatically credited to the 
Corporate / Institution account.  
d. Payment facility through internet banking  
 
(II) Transaction Entry 
Navigation:  Fee Collection -> Multiutility -> Multiutility Ref No Entry ->SCREEN 1900 MU: 
Reference Number Entry 
 
In the displayed screen, in the dropdown, the company/institution code is to be selected.  
On clicking “Transmit” the next screen is displayed with the parameterized particulars

In case of Function: Future payment, 
The Future Payment function is meant  for generation of reference number now and  to do  the 
financial transaction on a subsequent day. The financial transaction is to be completed within 10 
days of the reference number creation. 
 
Q will not generate and MUP No. will generate. The 1st two characters MU represent Multi Utility 
and  the  next  4  characters  represent  company/institution  code  and  the  remaining  characters

(III) Multiutility Collection of Fees using system generated challan through MUP at 
any of our branches  
The student will generate the challan by filling the template provided in the college website. On 
completion of the template an MUP reference number will be generated. The student has to take 
a  printout  using  the  reference  number.  The  pre-printed  challans  are  used  by  the  student  for 
remitting  the  fees.  Branches  should  not  alter  the  contents  of  the  challan  nor  the  amount  and 
should accept the amount as mentioned in the challan.  
Navigation:  Fee Collection -> Multiutility -> Multiutility Financial Transaction 
 
(IV) Fee Collection Module 
MIS details of the student shall be received from the institution and the details uploaded via the 
file upload screen 69004 as OFL file and the details will be fetched through the screen 1004. The 
details uploaded will be auto fetched in screen 1002 otherwise the details can be directly entered 
in screen 1002 and can complete the transaction.

transaction amount, click on TRANSMIT  
  
7.2  V- Collect 
IB V Collect suits the customer(beneficiary) who intends to receive funds from different sources 
through any bank branch counters/Netbanking facility. Collection is done primarily through NEFT 
and  if  thr remitter happens  to be our bank customer then  remittance can be accepted  by our 
branches through cash, transfer, cheque and BGL transfer. 
Customer will be allotted a Virtual company code (Ex. V00001). Customer will inform the remitter 
to give the beneficiary account number as Virtual company code(6 character)+ unique Remitter 
ID (Maximum of 15 characters) like dealer code/ subscription number etc..Chapter 7 FEE COLLECTION                                                                                                                        Page|  411  
    NBC User Manual V1.0

through any mode will be given credit to the mapped CBS account number of beneficiary

10.2 GEM  
Government  e-Marketplace  (GeM)  has  been  established  as  a  world  class  online  portal  for 
Government procurement. Banks have played an active role in facilitating various online services 
/ systems in the procurement cycle, including e-EMD, e- PBG, Gem Pool Account and other banking 
facilities / services that may be required by the buyers and vendors in the GeM portal from time 
to  time.  These  services  are  most  essential  for  making  Government  procurement  paperless, 
contactless and cashless.  
GeM pool Account is a special purpose bank account, operated and controlled exclusively by NPAE 
(Non-Public Financial Management System (PFMS) Agencies / Entities) for the purpose of crediting 
projected  value  of  the  contracts/supply  orders  in  to  the  account  and  for  subsequently  making 
timely payments to the suppliers on successful supply and acceptance of goods & services ordered 
through GeM against supply orders placed by NPAE on GeM.  In the case of Product Orders Buyers 
will make arrangements to credit 100% of the projected order value before the order creation.  
NPAE primary  user should register in GeM portal and  have to add GeM Pool Account details to 
ensure seamless payment. 
Real time details of all operations of the account will be shared by the bank, in a mutually accepted 
format (to be amended from time to time) with NPAE, only through the GeM platform.  
 
Requirements for opening new account: 
Bank has to manage two models for GeM Pool Account viz. Challan Model and Non-Challan Model. 
Accordingly, two Current Accounts styled as, CA-GENL_GeM Pool Account-INR under the product 
code 2311 – 1671 and CA-GENL GeM Pool Account – Non Challan under product code 2311-1971.  
 
Features of GeM Pool Account: 
This account will be used  by the NPAE exclusively  for crediting  the  funds and  subsequently for 
making payments to the seller’s account.  
The NPAEs are not permitted to use this GPA for any payment or operation outside GEM 
Once  the  transaction  specific  amount  is  credited  with  account,  the  NPAE  cannot  withdraw  this 
amount apart from transfer to the designated supplier, till such time the transaction is live. 
GPA would be utilised for all procurement of value above threshold limit defied by primary user. 
 
CBS screen is introduced to fund GeM Pool Account based on challan generated in GeM Portal by 
a  Buyer.  On  Providing  Challan  number  in  the  screen,  amount,  Account  Number  and  other 
necessary details will be pulled from GeM interface by consuming GeM Interface Web service. Once 
funding is success in CBS, the response will be send back to the GeM Interface. 
(Transfer/Cash/GL Transfer mode is enabled in CBS for funding GeM Pool Account)

10.4 TIN 2.0 
The Tax payment process is divided into three parts, challan preparation, payment authorization 
and CIN generation. 
Preparation of challan: 
The challan has to be generated by the Taxpayer through the TIN portal. The registered taxpayer 
has to login the TIN portal, where the basic details of the taxpayer like Name, address, mobile 
number, email and PAN/ TAN will be auto populated in the challan. The challan details has to be 
keyed in by the tax payer which will have mandatory fields required to be filled. All the details like 
challan number, major /minor head code, nature of payment and the breakup of tax details has 
to be updated by the tax payer. After the tax payer has finalized the challan, taxpayer will have 
to select preferred payment mode. The challan so generated will have a 14-digit (yymmdd followed 
by 08-digit) unique Challan Reference Number (CRN), assigned only when the challan is finally 
validated and generated. The CRN/challan so generated would be valid for a period of 15 days. 
TIN would purge all unused CRN on the day immediately after the date on which the validity period 
of 15 days is over.  
Once  payment  is  completed,  CIN  will  be  generated.  This  will  be  a  unique  18-digit  number 
containing 14-digit CRN generated by TIN portal for the challan and unique 4-digit Bank code. This 
CIN,  as  a  combination  of  CRN  and  Bank  Code,  will  be  reported  by  the  banks.  Such  CIN  is  an 
indicator of successful transaction and will be used as a key field for accounting, reconciliation and 
related mechanism by taxpayers. 
Over the Counter (OTC) Payment: 
While generation of challan in TIN  portal, when customer wants to do OTC payment he has to 
select the payment option as cheque, DD or cash payment. The name of the Authorized Bank and 
location where the instrument/ cash is to be presented should be filled in necessarily.  
Bank  CBS  will  have  an  interface  with  TIN  portal  and  the  details  of  the  CRN  generated  will  be 
transmitted to CBS on real time basis. On entering the CRN, the details filled by the taxpayer in 
TIN portal will be fetched. The teller should verify the details of the challan and should accept the 
receipt only when no discrepancy is found. Teller should also verify the payment mode and only 
accept the challan if it is over the counter payment. 
After accepting the challan, branch should feed the mobile number and select the mode of payment 
as cash / transfer / other bank cheque and feed the details. Once CIN is generated, this will be 
communicated to CBDT / TIN on real time basis. Hence, the transactions cannot be reversed.  
Important points to be taken care by branches:  
1. The transaction cannot be reversed  / corrected  once CIN  is generated, hence no correction 
menus are provided  
2. When payment is made through Other Bank Cheque, branches should ensure presenting the 
cheque in CTS the same day after generation of acknowledgement number in the TIN menu.

branch  has  to  effect  this  change  also  through  the  same  menu.  The  change  of  user  type  is 
permanent and is not revoked during the EOD and hence branch has to ensure that the changes 
are reversed, as and when the requirement is fulfilled. The concerned user ID should be in LOGOUT 
state.

Change signon branch permanently 
This menu is used when  
a. a staff gets transferred from one branch to another 
b. a staff is sent on deputation to another branch. 
 
The change signon branch permanently menu can be used only by the current home branch of the 
teller. The transaction would require authorisation by another officer of the branch. When a staff 
is transferred from one branch to another branch, the relieving branch should change the home 
branch of the staff from their branch to the branch to which he is transferred. Then only the staff 
would be able to log in as the user of the other branch. Similarly, when a staff is sent on deputation 
this menu should be used to change the signon branch, so that the deputed staff would be able to 
function from the other branch. When he is returned to his home branch, after deputation, the 
same menu should be used by the branch to reset his signon branch to his home branch. In case, 
a user is deputed to another branch during the day (after he has handled some transactions), it

This menu provides for changing the status of a teller as active or inactive and can be used for 
inactivating the user, when he goes on leave or is sent on an official duty or training. This would 
ensure that no other person can log into the system as that particular user when he is not available 
at the branch. Branch has to activate the teller when he returns to the branch after the leave or 
the official duty.  
 
The ‘change user status’ menu can also be used to make the User ID of a staff inactive,  
i) when he/she is suspended, 
ii) when he/she goes on sabbatical leave,  
iii) when the staff dies during the service and  
iv) On he/she retiring from the services of the Bank.  
v) On the retirement or death of a staff, CDC should be intimated, under System Mail, so 
that the user would be deleted from the system.

11.4 Mandate Management System 
National  Payments  Corporation  of  India  (NPCI)  introduced  National  Automated  Clearing  House 
(NACH) introduced  for Banks, Financial Institutions, Corporates & Government, is a web  based 
solution  to  facilitate  interbank,  high  volume,  electronic  transactions  which  are  repetitive  and 
periodic in nature. Mandate Management System (MMS) is a service of NACH debit which facilitates 
the process of Mandate creation, amendment and cancellation and offers all MIS related to the 
mandate.  
Credit ECS (Debit other bank accounts and receive credit for accounts with us):  
 Customer has to fill up the mandate form as prescribed by NPCI. 
 Branches have to verify all the details provided by the customer along with signature and 
accept three copies of the mandate forms.  
 Branches should enter the data in the CBS menu. On passing the queue, CBS system will 
generate the Bank Unique Reference Number (BURN), which should be noted in all the three 
copies of the mandate forms.  
 Branches have to handover first copy to the customer and  retain  the other copy in their 
record. Third copy of the Mandate form must be sent to Service Branch Chennai for scanning.  
 All the Mandates accepted by the destination banks will be provided with a Unique Mandate 
Reference  Number  (UMRN),  which  has  to  be  noted  in  the  Mandate  copy  retained  by  the 
branch after verifying the “Report of Accepted Mandate”. 
 Branches have to obtain the  fresh mandate from  the customers for all the  rejected/non-
accepted mandates and present it again as like as new mandate.Chapter 11 ADMINISTRATIVE MENU                                                                                                               Page|  517
